description: Editorial-typography event card system for Loop Lab workshops. Print-inspired grid with kicker, rule lines, three-column metadata, footer CTA, and an optional format badge. Fixes the loop-01 contrast defect and demonstrates a second event variant.

changelog.reason: loop-01 measured 2.49:1 against paper (fails WCAG AA 4.5:1 for normal text and 3:1 for large text). loop-02 value measures 5.28:1, clearing AA with margin. Fixing the token repairs all five dependent text nodes (metadata labels x3, edition tag, microcopy) at once, rather than patching each instance separately.

New reusable component demonstrating system extensibility: a right-aligned pill indicating event delivery format (e.g. Hybrid, Virtual, In-Person). Optional per event; omitted entirely in the in-person variant to show the grid degrades gracefully without it.

ink.role: primary text, title, values, logo mark, CTA fill, frame stroke

ink.muted.role: secondary/caption text: metadata labels, edition tag, microcopy

accent.role: kicker text, decorative brand rhythm marks, format badge fill

line.default.role: decorative rule lines and column dividers (non-text, not subject to text contrast gate)

on-ink.role: text on filled ink/accent surfaces (CTA button, format badge)

font.sans.role: kicker, labels, edition tag, CTA, logo mark, format badge

microcopy.position: bottom-left, aligned to CTA baseline row

formatBadge.position: top-right, right-aligned to content edge (x=1120), below header rule
formatBadge.usage: Shown for delivery formats worth flagging (Hybrid, Virtual). Omitted for standard in-person events, e.g. the loop-01 configuration below, to prove the grid does not depend on its presence.

eventVariants.description: Two event configurations proving the token system and grid generalize beyond a single hardcoded card. Both consume the same components/typography/spacing tokens above; only the content fields (and formatBadge.optional flag) differ per variant.
